name: French Onion Soup
recipeIngredient: 2 Pounds Onions (about 7 cups), thinly sliced (Vidalia are best)
2 Quarts Beef Stock, fresh or canned
1 Cup Imported Swiss Cheese, freshly grated OR combination of
recipeInstructions:
1. In a heavy saucepan or soup kettle, melt the butter and oil over moderate heat.
2. Stir in the onions and salt. Cook uncovered over low heat for 20-30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the onions are a rich golden brown.
3. Sprinkle flour over the onions and cook, stirring, for 2-3 minutes.
4. Remove the pan from the heat.
5. In a separate saucepan, bring the stock to a simmer. Stir in the hot stock into the onions. Return the soup to low heat and simmer, partially covered, for another 30-40 minutes, occasionally skimming off the fat.
6. Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper as needed.
Crotes:
1. Preheat the oven to 325°F.
2. Arrange bread slices in one layer on a baking sheet. Bake for 15 minutes.
3. Brush both sides of each slice lightly with olive oil using a pastry brush.
4. To serve, place crotes in a large tureen or individual soup bowls. Ladle the soup over them. Pass grated cheese separately.
Alternative:
1. Preheat the oven to 375°F.
2. Pour soup into an ovenproof tureen or individual soup bowls. Top with crotes and spread grated cheese on top.
3. Sprinkle cheese with a little melted butter or olive oil. Bake.
